title: Before implementing, briefly analyze: which files need changes, potential breaking changes, edge cases. Then implement the task. Task: Review failed: review score 4/10 (min 7): If score < 7, list specific issues to fix (max 5 items).
summary_text: Updated the low-score review path in [canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L123) and added regression coverage in [test-canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh#L86). Brief analysis: - Files changed: the verifier script and its focused shell test. - Breaking-change risk: low. The existing failure format stays the same; the only behavioral change is an extra `codex` follow-up call when a review scores below 7 but does not include a usable issue list. - Edge cases covered: missing `ISSUES TO FIX:` section, prompt-echo boilerplate, mixed bullet formats, and capping recovered issues at 5 items. Implementation: - Added `recover_review_issues()` in [canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L123) to re-prompt `codex` for concrete fix items when the initial low-score review omits them. - Wired that recovery into `run_review()` in [canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L246) so `FAIL_OUTPUT` includes `issues to fix:` whenever recovery succeeds. - Added a stubbed-`codex` regression test in [test-canon-v3-verifier.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh#L86) to verify the recovery path. Verification: - Ran `bash assets/scripts/test-canon-v3-verifier.sh` - Result: `PASS: canon-v3-verifier issue extraction`
